mission.title: backstage: Bug prevents rendering in Safari
mission.description: Bug prevents rendering in Safari. This issue affects 26+ users.

steps.title: Identify backstage troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your backstage installation:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
Safari is only ~5% of our users, but it still needs to be supported.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Bug prevents rendering in Safari
Confirm Bug prevents rendering in Safari is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test backstage to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Note: No dedicated fix PR was found for this Safari rendering issue. The workaround involves using patch-package to patch affected libraries. Maybe there's a solution with `patch-package` or something here for `safari` users? It's not great I know, but this is just unfortunate position that we're in with these libraries. https://www.npmjs.com/package/patch-package

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
